content.full_text:
RAvI MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
October 16,
1995
Orry Korb
Gait City Attorney
380 Civic Drive
Gait,
CA
95632
Re:
Your Request fOr Advice
Our File No.
A-95-328
Dear Mr.
Korb:
This is in response to your letter requesting advice on
behalf of Gait City Councilmember Robert C.
Kraude regarding his
responsibilities under the conflict-of-interest provisions of the
Political Reform Act
(the “Act”) •1
Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place.
In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us.
The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Ocilesby
(1975)
1 FPPC Ops.
71.)
QUESTION
May Councilmember Kraude participate in the Gait City
Council’s decision to provide financial assistance to the Gait
Joint Union High School District for the completion of a gymnasium
at Gait High Sch-ool where the councilmember teaches?
1
Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission
regulations appear at
2 California Code of Regulations,
Sections
18000—18995.
All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations.
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660


Our File No.
A-95-328
Page
2
CONCLUSION
Councilmember Kraude may participate in consideration of a
request from the Gait Joint Union High School District for
financial assistance the district needs to complete the gymnasium
project.
Pursuant to your facts,
Gait Joint Union High School
District is not considered an economic interest of the
councilmember for purposes of the disqualification provisions of
the Act.
FACTS
Councilmember Kraude is a member of the Gait City Council.
In his private capacity, the councilmember has been employed by
the Gait Joint Union High School District
(the “district”)
since
1979 as a teacher at Gait High Schooi.
The district encompasses
the city and certain contiguous portions of the unincorporated
area in Sacramento and San Joaquin Counties.
Gait High School is
located in the City of Gait.
The district is currentiy invoived in the construction of a
new gymnasium at Gait High School.
However, the district recently
determined that there is a
$2 miiiion shortfall in the district’s
capital budget and that the shortfali may affect the completion of
the gymnasium project.
It is anticipated that the district may
approach the city council to request financial assistance for the
completion of the gymnasium.
If the city provides these funds,
their use would be limited to the gymnasium project,
and would not
go into teacher salaries.
The councilmember has asked whether he
may participate in the decision to provide financial assistance to
the district.
ANALYSIS
Section 87100 of the Act prohibits any public official from
making, participating in making,
or otherwise using his or her
official position to influence a governmental decision in which
the official has a financial interest.
Section 87103 specifies
that a public official has a financial interest in a decision if
it is .reasonabiy foreseeable that the decision will have a
material financial effect, distinguishable from the effect on the
public generally,
on the official or a member of his or heT
immediate family or on:
(a)
Any business entity in which the public
official has a direct or indirect investment worth
one thousand dollars
($1000)
or more.
(b)
Any real property in which the public
official has a direct or indirect interest worth
one thousand dollars
($1000)
or more.


Our File No.
A—95—328
Page
3
(C)
Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a commercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status,
aggregating two hundred fifty
dollars
($250)
or more in value provided to,
received by or promised to the public official
within
12 months prior to the time when the
decision is made.
(d)
Any business entity in which the public
official is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee,
or holds any position of management.
(e)
Any donor of,
or any intermediary or
agent for a donor of,
a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars
($250)
or more in value
provided to, received by,
or promised to the public
official within
12 months prior to the time when
the decision is made.
The amount of the value of
gifts specified by this subdivision shall be
adjusted biennially by the Commission to equal the
same amount determined by the Commission pursuant
to subdivision
(d)
of Section 89504.
Section 87103(a)—(e).
As a public official,
Councilmember Kraude may not
participate in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable that the
decision will have a material financial effect on any economic
interest noted above.
However, while the district pays the councilmember a salary,
Section 82030(b) (2)
excludes from the definition of “income”
salary and reimbursement for expenses or per diem reáeived from a
state,
local or federal government agency.
Moreover,
since a
local government agency is not an organization or enterprise
operated for profit, it is not a “business entity” for purposes of
Section 87103 Cd) •2
Consequently, absent some other economic interest as set
forth in Section 87103 that will be affected by the decision in
question,
Councilmember Kraude may participate in the decisions
2
Section 82005 defines “business entity” as an organization or
enterprise operated for profit,
including but not limited to a
proprietorship, partnership, firm, business trust,
joint venture,
syndicate,
corporation or association.


Our File No.
A—95-328
Page
4
concerning, the gymnasium project.
However, please note that our
advice is limited only to the Political Reform Act.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at
(916)
322—566O.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
John W. Wallace
Counsel, Legal Division
Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.
Alternatively,
copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the Commission at. a
cost of 1O per page.
